questions.question: In the Transformer base model, what is the value of d_model?
questions.gold_answer: The Transformer base model uses d_model = 512.

questions.question: How many attention heads are used in the Transformer base model?
questions.gold_answer: The Transformer base model uses 8 attention heads.

questions.question: What fraction of tokens are selected for masking in BERT pre-training?
questions.gold_answer: BERT masks 15% of input tokens during pre-training.

questions.question: What is one main reason LoRA is parameter-efficient compared with full fine-tuning?
references: LoRA is parameter-efficient because it freezes the pretrained model weights and learns only low-rank adaptation matrices.
Instead of updating all model parameters, LoRA trains small low-rank matrices while keeping the original weights fixed.

questions.question: How does BERT differ from previous unidirectional language models?
references: BERT is deeply bidirectional, conditioning on both left and right context simultaneously, unlike unidirectional language models.
Unlike left-to-right or right-to-left models, BERT pre-trains bidirectional representations using masked language modeling.

questions.question: Why did the Transformer architecture reduce dependence on recurrence?
references: The Transformer uses self-attention to model long-range dependencies in parallel, reducing reliance on recurrent computation.
By replacing recurrence with attention, the Transformer enables greater parallelization and captures dependencies efficiently.

questions.question: Does BERT use bidirectional context during pre-training?
questions.gold_answer: Yes, BERT uses bidirectional context during pre-training.

questions.question: Does LoRA introduce additional inference latency after training?
questions.gold_answer: No, LoRA does not introduce additional inference latency.

questions.question: Does the Transformer rely on recurrence in its core architecture?
questions.gold_answer: No, the Transformer does not rely on recurrence in its core architecture.
